race.comment: MISS REVEALING (1) is hard to ignore here first up. Three runs in her maiden campaign read very well, breaking her maiden dominantly at start two, pitched up into the G3 Vanity at Flemington before a spell and finishing a three-length fifty to star filly Sheza Alibi. She draws ideally off some good trial form and might just be too good for these. PALTROW MISS (3) is a NZ filly who comes across having won her only start dominantly at New Plymouth Raceway. Have to respect the strong connections and the Maher/Williams combination. GOLD COAST BELLE (7) was far too good first up at Seymour, coming away to win by 2.25 lengths. That race fitness could prove very beneficial. ECO FORCE (5) is racing well without winning and has each-way claims again while BEAUTIFULLY (4) got the job done last time and should continue to improve.
